reviews.review_title: Body of Mine for an LGBTQ+ Center
reviews.review_description:
My director and I had the pleasure of meeting Cameron at a conference in Chicago last year. After doing the experience we automatically thought that our center would benefit from being able to show people the experience. Working with Cam was amazing and they were able to assist us in getting the headset to be able to bring it to our community. 

Our parents of trans kids group appreciated getting a better understanding of their kids’ experiences with gender dysphoria and euphoria. It sparked a lot of good conversations about medical transition being just another part of the way bodies can change and grow. 

Our trans and nonbinary staff have also enjoyed getting to play with and learn more about our feelings around gender dysphoria. I loved the stories and artistry. I felt so much euphoria seeing myself as a body of light. 

Much love from LBU!!

Come and experience the different body types and gender, while listening to the narratives of people who experience body and gender dysphoria. This program has the power to increase your understanding and empathy towards those who feel that they are born in the wrong body. 

It reaffirms your gender experiences whether you are cisgender, transgender or non-binary. It reminds me that I am connected of - different body parts and each of them have their own stories to them. Most importantly, all those parts are capable of growing and develop into something beautiful. 

I can keep going back to this program and not feel tired of experiencing each avatar. Can’t wait for more versions!
Thank YOU  for the amazing experience. Seeing the various avatars and body types. I loved every bit of it. BLACK PRIDE 🤎

This really puts you in the shoes, or more accurately in the body and mindset, of trans and gender non-conforming people. An incredible resource for anyone who wants to better understand gender dysphoria.

An exercise in empathy that you remember with your whole body.

Gorgeous rendering of a deeply personal experience that does not feel exploitative in the slightest.

On the contrary, it lifts up the participant and reminds us that the things that make us different are what make our communities strong.

Looking forward to seeing what comes next!

It is an immersive journey of self-exploration and empathy. The level of detail and the interactive elements make you feel like you're truly connecting with your own body and mind in ways that are profound and enlightening. 

I highly recommend this to anyone looking to explore themselves or gain a deeper understanding of others. It's not just an experience—it's a transformation. Well done to the creators! 👏
Very clever use of avatars for the purposes of embodiment and I loved the extra feature at the end where you can leave a recording to encourage others.
Affirming for people who have experienced gender dysphoria and very informative for people who haven't!

I'm a true believer that VR will be a powerful vehicle for learning in a fun and interesting way, and this experience shows the beginnings of that. The hand tracked interactions are a bit finicky (and it's only a little bit better with the controllers), but it is still a great experience.

This is a great way of putting you in somebody else's shoes in order to try to understand them better. Hoping future experiences from this developer can be a little bit less finicky, but overall still very much recommend this to everyone to try!

Just 15 minutes of standing in front of a mirror hearing gender dysphoria quotes I've heard a million times by now, "my skin is like sandpaper". It doesn't help that it's the same experience every time despite every person being different.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharing your thoughts. While you might have heard these stories before, not everyone has, and we purposefully included a range of voices from dozens of individuals to highlight that everyone's experience is different and unique.

It's meant to be a sandbox experience where you can explore at your own pace. I’m sorry to hear you only spent 15 minutes with it, as there's a lot more to uncover if you give it more time. We're constantly looking to improve and your feedback is super valuable to us.

reviews.review_description: I have seen a lot of VR experiences but nothing like this. The use of body and face tracking to tell such an intimate story is so beautifully done. While the tracking isn’t always perfect the soul of the experience shines bright. The story is incredibly impactful and everyone with a VR headset should try it

I'm so happy I'm able to experience this and I really wish everyone could. It's the most beautiful experiences I have on my headset and its just a 10 out of 10. I saw a clip of this on tiktok and I immediately went out and bought it even though I was on vacation and couldn't even play it for a few days. I truly believe this is the future and I am more than likely to have my friends go through it and maybe even my family when the time is right. Thank you to the people who contributed to this, I hope all of you nothing but the best.
Users of Meta Quest2 can't view it!

developer_response.body: Hello, Body of Mine does support Quest 2 and is verified to be working. Please make sure your firmware is up to date and try reinstalling the app. If you still have trouble you can contact hello@kost.ai for support!

reviews.review_description:
The only thing I was disappointed by this game is it made it look like you could fly around on the inside of the body, but you can’t.
You can only stand and look at a mirror and look around you and that’s it 
But aside from that hearing the peoples testimony of their experiences was all really great  👍👍

It’s difficult to come across authentic VR storytelling experiences, Body of Mine is that and much more! It’s a human experience that breaks down conventional narrative with incredible set design and immersive audio. A must try!!
Stories matter. Will update more when I can.
